title: Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Mandir, Ayodhya
intro: The Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Mandir in Ayodhya, Uttar Pradesh, is a grand and sacred temple dedicated to Lord Shri Ram.
description:
Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Mandir, Ayodhya
Introduction
The Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Mandir in Ayodhya, Uttar Pradesh, is a grand and sacred temple dedicated to Lord Shri Ram. This site is considered the center of immense reverence and faith in Hinduism as the birthplace of Lord Shri Ram.
Ayodhya is one of India's most significant religious pilgrimage destinations, where millions of devotees from across the country and abroad visit to have darshan (sight) of Lord Shri Ram.
Temple Information
 Temple Name: Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Mandir
 Other Names: Ram Mandir Ayodhya, Ayodhya Ram Mandir
 Location: Ram Janmabhoomi Path, Sai Nagar, Ayodhya, Uttar Pradesh 224123, India
 Presiding Deity: Lord Shri Ram (Ram Lalla)
 Nearest Major City: Lucknow (approx. 135 km)
History and Significance
Ayodhya is regarded as the birthplace of Lord Shri Ram, and this location holds a special place in Indian religious and cultural traditions.
The Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Mandir is dedicated to the child form of Lord Shri Ram, known as Ram Lalla. The temple stands as a center of faith and devotion for millions of devotees.
How to Reach
✈️ By Air
 Maharshi Valmiki International Airport, Ayodhya Dham: Approx. 10 km from the temple.
 Chaudhary Charan Singh International Airport, Lucknow: Approx. 135 km from the temple.
🚆 By Train
 Ayodhya Dham Junction: Approx. 2 km from the temple.
 Ayodhya Junction: Approx. 3 km from the temple.
🛣️ By Road
Ayodhya is well-connected by road to major cities like Lucknow, Prayagraj, Varanasi, Gorakhpur, and others. Facilities for buses, taxis, and private vehicles are readily available to reach the city.
Best Time to Visit
The period from October to March is considered the best time to visit Ayodhya as the weather is pleasant.
Special Occasions:
 Ram Navami
 Diwali
 Deepotsav
 Vivah Panchami
Temple Timings
 Morning: 6:00 AM – 12:00 PM
 Afternoon/Evening: 1:00 PM – 10:00 PM
(Note: Please verify the current darshan timings and guidelines before your visit.)
Nearby Religious Places
 Hanuman Garhi
 Kanak Bhawan
 Sita Ki Rasoi
 Nageshwarnath Temple
 Saryu River Ghat
Facilities for Devotees
 Organized Darshan arrangements
 Comprehensive security measures
 Help centers
 Nearby hotels and Dharmashalas (guesthouses)
 Local transport accessibility

faq.q1: Where is the Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Mandir located?
faq.a1: The Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Mandir is located in Ayodhya, Uttar Pradesh.
faq.q2: Who is the presiding deity of the Ram Mandir?
faq.a2: The child form of Lord Shri Ram, Ram Lalla, is worshipped in the temple.
faq.q3: Which is the nearest railway station to the Ayodhya Ram Mandir?
faq.a3: Ayodhya Dham Junction is the nearest major railway station.
faq.q4: What is the best time to visit the Ram Mandir?
faq.a4:
The period from October to March is considered ideal for a visit.
Conclusion
The Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Mandir in Ayodhya is not just a temple; it is a profound center of faith, devotion, and spirituality for millions. It remains one of the most important pilgrimage sites in India for the darshan of Lord Shri Ram.
